sourcePrompt: An M16A1, the standard US infantry rifle of the Vietnam War, about a meter long and lying level. A slim straight-line rifle: a triangular front sight tower above the muzzle, a fixed carry handle housing the rear sight, ribbed cylindrical handguards around a thin barrel, a magazine curving slightly forward below the receiver, a pistol grip, and a solid straight buttstock. Matte black polymer and dark gunmetal, clean hard military edges.
handEdit: mag rebuilt smooth: 3 chord-matched positive-angle segments (3/9/15deg) + floor plate; old -0.08/-0.16/-0.24 were radians flattened to ~0deg leaving an offset staircase
